honoree.name: Floyd B. Olson
honoree.summary: Floyd B. Olson was an American politician born on November 13, 1891, in Minneapolis, Minnesota. He served as the Governor of Minnesota from 1931 to 1936, known for his progressive policies and leadership during the Great Depression. Olson was a prominent figure in the Farmer-Labor Party and significantly shaped the state's political landscape. He died on August 22, 1936, in Rochester, Minnesota, from stomach cancer.

honoree.name: John A. Johnson
honoree.summary: John Albert Johnson was an American politician who served as the 16th Governor of Minnesota. He was born on July 28, 1853, in St. Peter, Minnesota Territory. Johnson died on September 21, 1904, in St. Peter, Minnesota. He is remembered for his progressive reforms and for being the first governor of Minnesota born within the state's borders.

honoree.name: C. Elmer Anderson
honoree.summary: C. Elmer Anderson was an American politician who served as the 29th Governor of Minnesota from 1951 to 1955. Born on March 31, 1912, in Detroit Lakes, Minnesota, he was a member of the Republican Party. His political career also included service in the Minnesota Senate. He died on January 24, 1998, in Brainerd, Minnesota.

highways.name: State Trooper Theodore "Ted" Foss Memorial Highway

honoree.name: Shawn Silvera
honoree.summary: Police Officer Shawn Silvera was struck and killed while deploying stop sticks on I-35W in an attempt to stop a vehicle being pursued by officers. The pursuit had started in Chisago County when officers attempted to serve a felony warrant on a man wanted in South Dakota. Officer Silvera had just placed the stop sticks on the roadway when the driver of the vehicle swerved around them and struck him. Officer Silvera had positioned himself on the median of the highway. After striking Officer Silvera, the vehicle continued into the northbound lanes and struck an oncoming vehicle. The suspect then attempted to flee on foot but was taken into custody. The man was charged with second degree murder in connection with Officer Silvera's death. He subsequently plead guilty and was sentenced to serve 42 years. The man died in prison on November 1st, 2017. Officer Silvera had served with the Lino Lakes Police Department for seven years. He is survived by his wife and two children.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Vehicular assault Weapon: Automobile Offender: Died in prison

honoree.name: Walter F. Mondale
honoree.summary: Walter Mondale was born on January 5, 1928, in Ceylon, Minnesota, and served as the 42nd Vice President of the United States under President Jimmy Carter from 1977 to 1981. He was a U.S. Senator from Minnesota and the Democratic Party's nominee for President in 1984, where he lost in a landslide to Ronald Reagan. Mondale had a long career in public service, including appointments as Ambassador to Japan under President Bill Clinton. He died on April 19, 2021, in Minneapolis, Minnesota, leaving a legacy as a key figure in late 20th-century American politics.
honoree.education: University of Minnesota, University of Minnesota Law School

highways.name: Deputy John W. Liebenstein Memorial Highway

honoree.name: John W. Liebenstein
honoree.summary: Deputy Sheriff John Liebenstein was killed by a vehicle that rammed his patrol car at 90 mph during a pursuit. Deputy Liebenstein was blocking an interstate ramp so the car, driven by a 17-year-old, could not exit the interstate. The juvenile was taken into custody after being treated at a local hospital.  He was convicted of first-degree murder and was given a mandatory sentence of life in prison without the possibility of parole. in 2018, he was granted the ability for parole. Deputy Liebenstein had served with the Rice County Sheriff's Department for nine years. He was survived by his wife and three children. 25 miles of I-35 have been dedicated as the Deputy Sheriff John W. Liebenstein Memorial Highway.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Vehicle pursuit Weapon: Automobile Offender: Apprehended

honoree.name: Trooper Glen Skalman
honoree.summary: Trooper Glen Skalman succumbed to gunshot wounds sustained 10 days earlier while making a traffic stop on US 61, two miles north of Forest Lake. Trooper Skalman had stopped a vehicle on Highway 61 for erratic driving. The suspect was seated in the passenger seat of the squad car while Trooper Skalman was writing the ticket. The suspect pulled out a gun and suddenly shot the trooper 5 times, including three times in the face. The suspect was identified quickly as the trooper was still conscious when the first responding officer arrived, and Skalman told him to look at the ticket pad where he had written down the suspect's license plate number. Despite multiple surgeries, Trooper Skalman died 10 days later, never regaining consciousness. The subject was later apprehended and sentenced to 40 years in prison after pleading guilty to second-degree murder. He was released after only serving seven years of his sentence. Trooper Skalman had served with the Minnesota State Patrol for three years. He is survived by his wife, daughter, and two sons. In 2014, U.S. Highway 10 in Minnesota was named Trooper Glen Skalman Memorial Highway.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Gunfire Incident Date: Thursday, December 17, 1964 Weapon: Handgun; .22 caliber Offender: Sentenced to 40 years

honoree.name: Corrections Officer Joseph Gomm
honoree.summary: Corrections Officer Joseph Gomm was beaten to death by an inmate at Minnesota Correction Facility–Stillwater at approximately 1:30 p.m. He was supervising inmates in the prison's industry building when he was attacked by one inmate with a hammer. Officer Gomm was transported to Regions Hospital, in St. Paul, where he succumbed to his injuries. Officer Gomm had served with the Minnesota Department of Corrections for 16 years. He is survived by his mother, three brothers and two sisters. In August 2019, a portion of MN state highway 95, running from Interstate 94 in Lakeland to MN state Highway 36 in Oak Park Heights, was renamed Corrections Officer Joseph Gomm Memorial Highway.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Assault Weapon: Blunt object; Hammer Offender: In custody
